question: How to set an off-center subject mode of a camera?
content:
Use focus lock in One-Shot AF.
1. On the lens, set the focus mode switch to <AF>. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode, press <AF-WB>, and turn the Main Dial until ONE SHOT is selected.
2. Press the AF point selection button and choose the desired AF point with the Multi-controller, Main Dial, or Quick Control Dial; the selected point appears in the viewfinder and LCD panel.<PIC>
3. Put that AF point over the off-center subject and press the shutter button halfway to focus.<PIC>
4. Keep the shutter button halfway pressed, recompose the picture, then press it fully to take it.<PIC>
Focus lock works only in One-Shot AF. It does not work in AI Servo AF or AI Focus AF after Servo mode is active. In Basic Zone modes except A-DEP, you can also use focus lock by starting from placing the AF point over the subject.
ret:
"Use focus lock in One-Shot AF.
1. On the lens, set the focus mode switch to <AF>. Set the Mode Dial to a Creative Zone mode, press <AF-WB>, and turn the Main Dial until ONE SHOT is selected.
2. Press the AF point selection button and choose the desired AF point with the Multi-controller, Main Dial, or Quick Control Dial; the selected point appears in the viewfinder and LCD panel.<PIC>
3. Put that AF point over the off-center subject and press the shutter button halfway to focus.<PIC>
4. Keep the shutter button halfway pressed, recompose the picture, then press it fully to take it.<PIC>
Focus lock works only in One-Shot AF. It does not work in AI Servo AF or AI Focus AF after Servo mode is active. In Basic Zone modes except A-DEP, you can also use focus lock by starting from placing the AF point over the subject.",["Manual10_104", "Manual10_106", "Manual10_107"]
notes: Answered from the Canon EOS 20D AF mode, AF point selection, and focus-lock procedure. Three <PIC> placeholders align with Manual10_104, Manual10_106, and Manual10_107.
